title: Eyes on arts: Upcoming entertainment highlights
bodyText:
GEORGE ENTERTAINMENT NEWS - Georgians can pick and choose their weekend entertainment from a large variety of events.

Below is our weekly calendar for the arts: Eyes on arts.


> These entries are sent to George Herald on a weekly basis. Have a look and book your tickets for some stunning entertainment on your doorstep.


15 August:

One Night in Spain (18:30) at the Market Community Theatre. Flamenco dance by Marlise’s School of Spanish Dance. Book a ticket for R150 per person on Webtickets.

Watch these ladies in action and support a great cause - their upcoming trip to Spain! Photo: SAB Photography

29 August - 6 September:

Little Women at the George Arts Theatre. Experience this timeless classic as the March sisters, Meg, Jo, Amy and Beth, navigate life, love and growing up during the American Civil War.

A heartfelt journey for all generations. Tickets available on wwwgeorgeartstheatre.co.za.


> Scenes from the Opera, 26 September (18:30) at Sedgefield NG Church and 28 September (16:00) at Market Community Theatre.


Presented by Goldblatt Music Productions, featuring Phyllis Goldblatt (soprano), Siphesihle Mdena (tenor), Luvo Rasemeni (bass-baritone), and Zolina Ngejane (mezzosoprano), with piano accompaniment by Vanessa Phillips.

Tickets are R160 and available at the Sedgefield NG Church Office or at the door, and from Quicket or Heather (072 369 3304) for the production in George.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
